summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox focused on enhancing compatibility and reliability on macOS platforms. The changes include the re-enabling of several important test types, such as mochitests for screen sharing, which is critical for ensuring the stability of video calls and other real-time communication features. Additionally, crashtests, which help identify when the browser crashes under specific conditions, were re-enabled and optimized. These adjustments are essential to maintain a smooth and reliable experience for users engaging in WebRTC activities, ensuring that technology behind **video calls and online collaboration tools** works seamlessly on newer macOS versions.
